The Government's Decisions:

Petition No. 6145/2025 In this court case, Md. Pursuant to the order of the High Court dated 04.12.2025, "Vadegaon, Ta. The petitioner for the point Balapur, G. Akola is Education Broadcasters Board, Wadegaon, Ta. Balapur, Dist. Akola Institute of Management, Sri Jageshwar College of Arts, Commerce and Science, Wadegaon, Ta. The newly proposed college at Balapur, G. Akola is being granted a permanent non-granting certificate from the academic year 2026-2027 subject to the following terms and conditions as provided in Section 109 (3) (A) of the Maharashtra Public University Act, 2016 as mentioned in the following statement. In this letter, Shri. The High Court, Bombay Bench filed a petition at Nagpur No. Approval is being granted subject to the final judgment in the 3314/2025: -
Terms and Conditions: -
The following terms and conditions shall apply to the aforesaid letters of intent: -
1) As per the prevailing policy of the Government, the Letter of Intent to start a new college shall remain for the purpose of starting a college on an unaided basis.

2) Since the letter of intent is for the purpose of fulfilling the prescribed terms and conditions before the institution receives final approval for the new college, the letter of intent does not enable the institution to start a college unless it receives the final approval of the government to start a new college.
table_trans: Name of the institution and college
Education Broadcasting Board, Wadegaon, Ta. Balapur, Dist. Akola, Sri Jageshwar Arts, Commerce & Science | College, Wadegaon, Ta. Balapur,
BA (Faculty of Humanities). BCom (Faculty of Commerce and Management). BSc (Faculty of Science and Technology).

para_trans: 3) The letter of intent issued by the government for starting a new college as per Section 109 (3) (S) of the Maharashtra Public Universities Act, 2016 shall be valid till 31st January, 2027. During this period, it will be mandatory for the university to submit a compliance report through the new College Accreditation System (NCPS) along with the documents required for final approval, meeting the terms and conditions on which the letter of intent has been issued.

The procedure for final recognition is clearly outlined in the Maharashtra Public Universities Act and under the Government Decision Date 18.09.2025. As per the provisions of the Act, b. Institutions receiving a certificate of intent as per the said procedure meet the terms and conditions of the letter of intent, they shall apply to the University for final approval through the new College Accreditation System (NCPS) and the Universities shall submit the application of all eligible applicant institutions along with the compliance report to the Government through the new College Accreditation System (NCPS) along with the recommendations for approval.

4) The letter of intent shall be deemed to have lapsed if the management fails to comply with the condition of the letter of intent within the time limit specified in clause (e) as specified in the proviso under section 109 (3) (f) of the Maharashtra Public Universities Act, 2016.
5) The university concerned should not initiate the process of affiliation until the college has received the final recognition.

6) The college is being issued a letter of intent to start a college of the above mentioned courses on the assumption that it is an official course of the university concerned. The university must verify this.
7) The college should not, under any circumstances, admit students on the basis of a letter of intent.
If it is found that such admission has been made, in accordance with the provisions of the law relating to
The ANAS will be done and the will will be cancelled.

8) While providing facilities in the college, facilities will be created for the differently abled student as per the provisions of The Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 20196 as well as the norms prescribed in this regard from time to time.
This should be verified and while submitting a compliance report for final approval.
It will be mandatory to submit a certificate in this regard.

9) It shall be mandatory to comply with the provisions of Higher and Technical Education, Government Decision, dated 25.07.2013 with respect to implementation of the State Code and Youth Policy, 2012.
3. While taking further action as may be required by the University regarding the grant of final recognition.
Follow the procedure B criteria prescribed under the government decision in question.
